mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month momentum in industrial production raises expected near-term earnings for cyclicals and broad index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO 3-month momentum does not produce positive 20-day return spreads versus lower momentum states, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 2.0% tightens the discount-rate environment and reduces equity valuation support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices are rising and the federal funds rate is also rising, inflation pressure forces tighter policy rather than growth support, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rising-CPI and rising-FEDFUNDS state fails to underperform all other states over 60 trading days in walk-forward tests, or if interaction term t-stat is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion with unemployment below 5.5% signals resilient household income and spending capacity, raising expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the low-unemployment payroll-growth state does not deliver higher 20-day average returns than other labor states, or if hit rate for positive returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1000 thousand units indicate weakening interest-sensitive demand and construction activity, reducing forward growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1000 triggers produce nonnegative 60-day excess returns versus unconditional returns in two consecutive walk-forward evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher short-run retail sales momentum indicates stronger current consumption and near-term revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS 1-month or 3-month momentum has a negative average 5-day forward return spread versus lower momentum states over the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with rising federal funds rates identifies a late-cycle policy restraint phase in which stronger activity brings tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the GDP-growth and rising-FEDFUNDS interaction has positive 60-day return alpha after controlling for each single series, or if interaction Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production alongside rising consumer prices signals an inflationary slowdown that compresses margins and limits monetary easing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflationary-slowdown observations do not have lower 20-day returns than nontriggered observations, or if downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.
